features_valued:
📝 Notes
Aug 28, 2025
Kayson User Test
Invited Cam Watkins kaysonlconlin@gmail.com harmon.justin@gmail.com
Attachments Kayson User Test 
Meeting records Transcript Recording 
Summary
Justin Harmon interviewed Kayson L Conlin to gather information for their research, focusing on Kayson L Conlin's career journey, job hunt experiences, and tools used for resume management

Kayson L Conlin shared their positive experiences with Teal, an AI-powered service that tailors resumes to job requirements and helps with cover letters, highlighting its effectiveness in boosting confidence and generating more attention from employers

Kayson L Conlin praised Teal's ease of use and ability to parse existing resumes but suggested that a missing feature is the ability to pull information from a wider range of websites for job applications

Details
Introduction and Career Journey Justin Harmon began the meeting by introducing themself to Kayson L Conlin and stating the purpose of the recorded research interview, which was to gather information for their research (00:00:00)

Kayson L Conlin shared their career journey, detailing their five years of on-and-off product and UX design experience, their background in visual design and marketing, and their recent role as a creative director where they were tasked with website redesign before being laid off (00:07:50)
They also mentioned their current position on the Tesla autopilot team, training AI for the Optimus humanoid robot, and clarified that their team is based in Draper, Utah, not Austin (00:09:46)
Job Hunt Pain Points and Resume Tools Justin Harmon inquired about Kayson L Conlin's job hunt experiences, specifically focusing on resume-related pain points and the tools they used
They then found a service called Teal, which uses AI to tailor resumes to job requirements by selecting and customizing bullet points and helping write cover letters (00:11:17)

Teal Features and Effectiveness Kayson L Conlin described Teal as a web app with a desktop app and Chrome extension that reads job postings, saves information to a job database, and assesses resume fit (00:12:59)
They explained that Teal aims for a 65-75% correlation between the job requirements and the resume to appear authentic yet optimized (00:14:08)

Resume Management in Teal Kayson L Conlin elaborated on how Teal manages resumes, explaining that users create a master copy with numerous bullet points for each job, and Teal then suggests which bullet points to include based on a job description to avoid overly long resumes

They also mentioned that Teal uses AI to generate bullet points and cover letters, which is more accurate than general chatbots like Claude or ChatGPT because it's based on the user's specific resume information (00:15:17)
Kayson L Conlin compared general AI to an "eager intern" that prioritizes impressing over accuracy (00:16:33)
Teal's Best and Missing Features Justin Harmon asked Kayson L Conlin to identify the best and most desired missing features of Teal

Kayson L Conlin praised Teal's ease of use, particularly its ability to parse and divide an existing design-focused resume into bullet points, despite previous challenges with visual resumes (00:17:34)
positive_mentions: They also mentioned that Teal uses AI to generate bullet points and cover letters, which is more accurate than general chatbots like Claude or ChatGPT because it's based on the user's specific resume information (00:15:17)
They also appreciated the minimal design options but wished for more personalization in templates (00:18:44)

Content and Role Management in Prototype Kayson L Conlin provided feedback on a prototype, noting that their expectation for an "add content" feature would be to include multimedia like portfolios or presentations, rather than just text editing (00:30:12)

They suggested that if the prototype could expand its current feature of pulling information from the internet to also auto-populate job applications, similar to Teal's functionality, it would be highly useful (00:40:10)
Kayson L Conlin emphasized the value of accurate field alignment for correct prepopulation, as they often use Teal to generate PDFs that auto-populate job applications (00:41:38)
Justin Harmon: Appreciate you hopping on with me today
Um but we're doing a little uh research interview and so I appreciate your time and appreciate you going through this with us
Um, unless you want to get it out of the way, but um, once I share the link and we start going through things, that's when, um, it'd be super helpful
Justin Harmon: Um, but I wanted to talk to you a little bit about like your job hunt during that time period
um specifically around kind of um some of your pain points around um your resume um your CV kind of and just tell me a little bit about how you uh maintain what tools you're using stuff like that

Kayson L Conlin: Um, I wouldn't say that's necessarily what got me where I'm at, but I definitely would say it helped to um it it it definitely helped boost my confidence and probably helped get me a little bit more attention and more um, you know, beyond just the initial interviews and a lot more follow-ups and stuff like that
Is it like a web app or Oh, they have a desktop thing, too

If if you're on a job site and it finds enough information that it says, "Oh, this must be a job posting." It will say, "Would you like me to, you know, would you like me to uh save this information to your job database?" And what it can do is it can go through, you know, you can go through several of these jobs and it'll tell you how good of a fit you are based on your resume and then it'll help you tailor your resume so that you can be an even better fit
and you're looking for a correlation on those key points, but they're looking for not just like a 100% correlation because that looks a little bit too artificial

So, you know, enough that it will stick out and people will say, "Hey, you can do these things, but not so much."
Justin Harmon: Yeah, that sounds like a powerful one
